summary: An identity is an equation that is true for every allowed value of the variable, not just for a few solutions. To prove one, you show the two sides are the same expression — here, by simplifying left − right and checking it reduces to 0.
method: Simplify it using algebra and known identities.
If the difference simplifies to 0, the identity holds for all values.
If it simplifies to something non-zero, it is not an identity (it may still be an equation with isolated solutions — use Solve for those).

example.title: Prove a Pythagorean identity
steps: By the Pythagorean identity sin²x + cos²x = 1, so the difference is 1 − 1 = 0.
